foundation.title: SELECTION
foundation.subtitle: Boolean Decision
foundation.content: Selection logic allows your Java program to choose between two paths. It works using the 'if/else' block, which depends on a condition that is either True or False.
foundation.scenario: A Digital Bouncer. If your 'age' variable is at least 21, the bouncer (Java) lets you into the code block. If not, you are denied entry.

examples.title: The Simple If
examples.explanation: Checking one single condition. If it is false, nothing happens.

examples.title: The Dual Path
examples.explanation: Defining what happens when the first check fails using 'else'.

examples.title: Comparison Ops
examples.explanation: Using math to create logic: == (equal), != (not equal), > (bigger).

foundation.title: ELSE_IF
foundation.subtitle: Multiple Outcomes
foundation.content: For more than two choices, Java uses 'else if'. The JVM checks them in order until it finds a True one, then skips the rest.
foundation.scenario: A Grade Calculator. If the score is 90 (A), the system finishes. It doesn't bother checking if it's 80 (B) or 70 (C).

examples.title: The Chain
examples.explanation: Rating results into multiple logical categories.
examples.code: if (x > 9) { high(); } else if (x > 5) { mid(); } else { low(); }

examples.title: Input Mapping
examples.explanation: Deciding which command to run based on a string character.

examples.title: Safety Nest
examples.explanation: Ensuring a default action happens if all other checks fail.

foundation.keyPoint: Only the FIRST true block in the chain will execute.

foundation.title: LOGIC
foundation.subtitle: Complex Conditions
foundation.content: Use '&&' (AND) and '||' (OR) to combine multiple checks into a single intelligent instruction.
foundation.scenario: A Bank ATM. To withdraw money, you must have the Correct PIN (AND) a Sufficient Balance (AND) the ATM must have Cash.

examples.title: The Double AND
examples.explanation: Both conditions must be True simultaneously.

examples.title: The Double OR
examples.explanation: The block runs if AT LEAST ONE part is True.

examples.title: Logical NOT
examples.explanation: The '!' symbol flips a True to a False. 'Not empty' means it has data.
